explanations.5: The backslash at the end of this line lets us continue the command on the next line, so we can keep related commands together and readable.
explanations.7: This removes cached package files that apt downloaded, which shrinks the Docker image size since we don't need them anymore.
explanations.8: This deletes the apt package lists to save more space in the image—Docker images should be as small as possible.
explanations.12: The -rm flags create the home directory and use bash as the shell; useradd is the command to add a new user account to the system.
explanations.13: We're piping the username and password into chpasswd, which is a tool that changes passwords by reading them from input.
explanations.16: sed is a text editor tool that finds and replaces patterns in files; the 's/' means substitute, and the ^ means start of line.
explanations.25: CMD tells Docker what command to run when the container starts; -D means sshd runs in the foreground so Docker can track it.
